nodes.label: Locked Decisions Table
nodes.rationale: Decisions frozen before coding/art because changing them later causes cascading rework across the whole project.

nodes.label: Base Resolution 640x360
nodes.rationale: Exact integer scaling to 720p (x2), 1080p (x3), 1440p (x4); changing later breaks all UI and framing.

nodes.label: Tile Size 16x16 px
nodes.rationale: Classic 16-bit style; all scenery art derives from this size, changing it means redoing all art.

nodes.label: Sprite Pivot: Bottom-Center
nodes.rationale: Required for Y-sort to work correctly (what is 'behind' renders first).

nodes.label: Texture Filter: Nearest
nodes.rationale: Nearest filter keeps pixel art crisp instead of blurred.

nodes.label: Movement Only in _physics_process
nodes.rationale: Mixing movement code with _process causes jitter.

nodes.label: Collision Layers (world, player, enemies, hurtboxes, pickups)
nodes.rationale: Physical body (blocking) and hurtbox (receiving damage) must always be separate areas; never reuse the body's CollisionShape for damage.

nodes.label: Input Actions Convention (move_click, skill_1..4, attack)
nodes.rationale: Code never references a physical key directly, only Input.is_action_pressed(name), so changing control scheme or adding gamepad support touches no scripts.

nodes.label: EventBus Autoload (global signals)
nodes.rationale: UI and systems listen to EventBus; entities emit. HUD must never hold a direct reference to the player node.

nodes.label: Stats Pipeline Formula ((base+flats)x(1+percents))
nodes.rationale: Defining the flat-vs-percent stacking formula upfront avoids the classic bug where a +10% item does not interact correctly with a +5 flat item.

nodes.label: Data as Resources (.tres, never hardcoded)
nodes.rationale: New content = new .tres file with no code changes; balancing becomes editing data files instead of hunting numbers in scripts.

nodes.label: Code Conventions (naming, unique names, one component per file)
Save System Plan (JSON at user://save.json)
nodes.rationale: Design stats/inventory/progress as serializable pure data from the start rather than scattered across nodes.

nodes.label: Pause System Plan (get_tree().paused + process_mode)
nodes.rationale: Set up correct process_mode on UI nodes from the first scene, otherwise it becomes a bug hunt later.

nodes.label: Single Color Palette Decision
nodes.rationale: Mixing palettes is what makes an indie game look amateur; choose one palette (e.g. DB32/Resurrect) before the first sprite.
